claims.claim: Moonshot AI was founded in March 2023 (legal entity registered 2023-04-17) by Yang Zhilin with co-founders Zhou Xinyu, Wu Yuxin, and Zhang Yutao
Yang Zhilin is first author of XLNet (arXiv 1906.08237) and second author of Transformer-XL (arXiv 1901.02860), CMU PhD 2019 under Ruslan Salakhutdinov
The company name comes from Pink Floyd's The Dark Side of the Moon, with founding timed to the album's 50th-anniversary year
Seed funding (2023) from HongShan and ZhenFund at a ~$300M valuation; reported raise size conflicts between ~$60M and ~$200M
February 2024: ~$1B round led by Alibaba at ~$2.5B post-money valuation, then the largest single round for a Chinese LLM startup
Alibaba disclosed in its FY2024 20-F that it invested ~US$0.8B (RMB5.9B) for an ~36% equity interest in Moonshot via preferred stock
Roughly half of Alibaba's $800M investment was paid as Alibaba Cloud computing credits rather than cash, per FT reporting
August 2024: $300M+ round with Tencent and Gaorong Capital at a $3.3B valuation
December 2025: $500M Series C led by IDG Capital at $4.3B post-money, oversubscribed by Alibaba and Tencent
May 2026: ~$2B raised at $20B post-money, led by Meituan's Long-Z Investments with Tsinghua Capital, China Mobile, CPE Yuanfeng; ~$3.9B raised over prior six months per Huafeng Capital
A new round opened ~June 30, 2026 at $31.5B pre-money (Bloomberg June 8 reported $30B target), unclosed as of July 17, 2026
ARR ~$100M (early March 2026) rose to $200M+ by April 2026 per Huafeng Capital, >$300M by mid-June; a Nomura call cited $400-500M — all unaudited and investor-sourced
Kimi chatbot launched October 9, 2023 with a 200,000-Chinese-character context claim; 2M-character beta announced March 18, 2024
Kimi app peaked above 36M MAU in October 2024 (company figure); QuestMobile measured China MAU falling from 21.653M (Q1 2025) to 9.027M (Q4 2025), a 58.3% decline
Kimi's paid user-acquisition spend was >700M RMB in 2024 (199M RMB in October 2024 alone, 16th among all Chinese apps), collapsing to ~150M RMB in Q1 2025 and under 100,000 RMB by Q4 2025
Moonshot halted 'money-burning' ad campaigns in mid-February 2025 amid the DeepSeek craze — the first of the AI tigers to cut
DeepSeek R1 and Kimi k1.5 launched the same day (2025-01-20); R1 shipped MIT open weights while k1.5 shipped only a technical report (arXiv 2501.12599)
In February 2024 Yang Zhilin said open-sourcing one's best model is what 'laggards' do — a position K2's July 2025 open-weights release reversed
Kimi K2 released July 11, 2025 as open weights under Modified MIT: 1T total / 32B active MoE, 15.5T tokens, MuonClip, zero loss spikes
K2's Modified MIT license requires products with >100M MAU or >$20M monthly revenue to prominently display 'Kimi K2' — attribution only, no royalties
K2 Thinking (Nov 6, 2025) scored 67 on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index — highest open-weights score at the time, second only to GPT-5
CNBC's $4.6M K2 Thinking training-cost figure (anonymous source) was disavowed by Yang Zhilin as 'not an official number'
Cursor's Composer 2 was built on Kimi K2.5 (leaked model ID), accessed via a paid Fireworks AI commercial partnership; Cursor says ~1/4 of final-model compute came from the base

Five of seven Recurrent AI investors (GSR, Jingya, Boyu, Huashan, Wanwu) filed HKIAC arbitration against Yang Zhilin and Zhang Yutao on 2024-11-11 over the un-waived Moonshot spin-out; tribunal formed ~Feb 2025; no public resolution as of July 2026

Zhu Xiaohu accused ex-GSR partner Zhang Yutong of concealing 9M free Moonshot founding shares (~14% initial equity, ~$100M); Yang Zhilin rebutted that her shares were vesting co-founder compensation; she was confirmed as Moonshot president in December 2025
Kimi K2 was trained on NVIDIA H800 GPUs (per the K2 technical report and the Nov 2025 AMA); Moonshot has never disclosed cluster size and says it is 'outnumbered' in high-end GPUs

Anthropic (Feb 23, 2026) attributed over 3.4M Claude exchanges via fraudulent accounts to Moonshot (16M total across ~24,000 accounts with DeepSeek and MiniMax); Moonshot has issued no public response

On April 29, 2026 the House Select Committee on the CCP and House Homeland Security Committee opened a joint investigation citing Cursor's Composer 2 as 'reportedly built on an open-weight model developed by Moonshot AI'
Moonshot AI is not on the US Entity List as of July 2026 (unlike Zhipu, added Jan 2025), though formal proposals to add it exist
Anthropic's May 28, 2026 Series H: $65B at $965B post-money on $47B run-rate revenue (~20.5x); Moonshot's $31.5B pre-money ask on ~$200-300M ARR implies ~100-158x
Zhipu listed on HKEX Jan 8, 2026 (raising ~$559M); FY2025 revenue RMB 724.33M with total losses of RMB 4.72B; its shares fell 28.49% on July 17, 2026 to a ~US$63B market cap
MiniMax listed on HKEX Jan 9, 2026 (+109% debut); by July 17, 2026 its market cap was ~US$8.7B, roughly 82% below its March 2026 peak
Moonshot told shareholders in May 2026 it would unwind its offshore VIE structure ahead of a Hong Kong IPO targeted for late 2026-2027
Moonshot headcount: ~40 (2023) → 100+ (Nov 2024) → 300 (Dec 2025 internal letter) → '300多人' (mid-2026, enterprise chief Huang Zhenxin)
Kimi K3 launched July 16, 2026: 2.8T parameters, 1M-token context, $0.30/$3.00/$15.00 per-Mtok pricing, weights promised by July 27, 2026, with a Kimi Enterprise tier
QuestMobile May 2026: Doubao 382M, Qwen 167M, DeepSeek 130M MAU in China; Kimi absent from the top tier
Kimi-K2-Instruct shows 228,819 rolling-month Hugging Face downloads as of July 17, 2026, a year after release; at launch it was the fastest-downloaded model on Hugging Face
At K2 launch Groq served the open weights at >400 output tok/s versus ~10 tok/s on Moonshot's first-party API — Moonshot distributes inference largely through third-party hosts
Company-sourced 2026 momentum: overseas API revenue 4x since Nov 2025; K2.5's first 20 days of monetization exceeded all of 2025 revenue; paying users growing >170% MoM — none independently verified
On July 17, 2026, K3's launch coincided with sharp listed-peer declines (Zhipu -28.49%, MiniMax -15.6%) amid a broader tech selloff and post-lockup supply
